rationale: Architecture/blast-radius/portability all point to C. BLAST RADIUS: C keeps FindOrphanedClosedBeads (orphans.go:74) as the byte-identical fail-open wrapper on gitutil.BranchExists for its three shared consumers (complete.go:111, doctor/orphaned_beads.go:16, next) AND deletes the new BranchExistsE show-ref three-way seam entirely — so it adds LESS execution-domain (gitutil) surface than A or B, not more. D is the only approach that changes the detection SHAPE (worktree-enumeration), forking a second predicate away from the one all four verbs share — the exact drift anti-pattern the spec's single-gate-home architecture (Non-Goals) exists to prevent; rejected outright. ADR-0030: C's fail-closed posture rests on exec.MergeBase("main",specBranch) at impl.go:249, which already lives in ApproveImpl (the enforcement package), not the executor — no I/O-boundary violation, no new git probe pushed into the wrong layer. PORTABILITY: verified NO git floor is documented (go.mod pins only Go 1.23.0; zero git-version refs in .github, README, docs, AGENTS.md). A's hard git>=2.43 requirement is a real user-facing regression on common LTS distros, and its older-git fallback re-imports the exact exit-code classification spiral A was meant to end. SPEC FIT: C applies the spec-114 'delete the classifier' lesson the spec itself cites, and fact-5 shows the un-gated merge is structurally impossible (MergeBase@:249 for whole-store; FinalizeEpic merge-fail/no-worktree for single-ref) — layered, not single-point. If the panel wants the store-health signal explicit rather than an incidental coupling to :249, promote to a minimal C+B (a cheap version-agnostic health assert co-located with the scan); either stays floor-free.

rejected.A: Introduces an undocumented hard git>=2.43 floor (verified absent) = user-facing portability regression; its older-git fallback reopens the classification spiral; and the new BranchExistsE show-ref seam is the largest execution-domain surface of the four.
rejected.B: Correct and version-agnostic, but the explicit second store-health probe adds gate surface that C already gets for free from the pre-existing MergeBase@:249 precondition — more machinery, no additional risk closed.
rejected.D: Changes the detection shape to worktree-enumeration, diverging from the shared FindOrphanedClosedBeads predicate that complete/next/doctor depend on — biggest blast radius, forks a second detector, and couples the scan to executor merge mechanics.
